toc.title: Book One: The Way of Kings
35: A LIGHT BY WHICH TO SEE

points.text: glowing ancient armour that grants enormous strength and seals against harm, paired with a Shardblade
Highprince and Elhokar's Highprince of Information, an old rival of Dalinar who runs the cruellest bridge crews
feigns alliance with Dalinar, then withdraws his army at the Tower to let the Parshendi destroy him
Kaladin's mother, quietly clever and unusually well read for a darkeyed woman of Hearthstone
Gavilar's son and king of Alethkar, insecure and quick to suspect assassins everywhere
his saddle girth is sabotaged during a hunt; he blames a plot, sowing distrust between the highprinces
the king's sharp-tongued jester who insults the powerful and speaks in riddles
shows Kaladin unexpected kindness on a dark night, hinting he is far more than a fool
a fabrial worn on the hand that transforms one substance into another, the prize Shallan means to steal
the recurring tempest that sweeps Roshar, dangerous enough to kill yet the source of all Stormlight
during it Dalinar falls into his visions, and many believe a presence rides within the storm

roshar.title: Roshar

points.text: a storm-scoured world where life clings to stone, plants pull in their tendrils and crustacean beasts roam
points.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: shaped by the highstorms that sweep east to west, leaving Stormlight and ruin in their wake

roshar.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: the deadliest, most cursed bridge crew in Sadeas's army, where men are sent to die
Kaladin turns them into a true unit, training them in secret and saving the wounded others leave behind
they buy their freedom with Dalinar and become his honoured guard under Kaladin

desolations.title: The Desolations

points.text: ancient cataclysms when the Voidbringers returned to scour humanity, held off by the Heralds and Radiants
points.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: Jasnah and Dalinar fear, from her research and his visions, that another Desolation is coming

desolations.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: a darkeyed surgeon's son from Hearthstone who once dreamed of healing, then chose the spear
sold into slavery and branded shash after defying a lighteyes; ends up a bridgeman in Sadeas's warcamp
takes over Bridge Four and forges its broken men into a brotherhood that survives the chasms
unknowingly draws in Stormlight to heal and fight, the first sign he is becoming a Windrunner
saves Dalinar's army at the Tower and earns Bridge Four their freedom, becoming captain of the guard
speaks the First Ideal: Life before death, strength before weakness, journey before destination
a sheltered lighteyed girl of impoverished House Davar from Jah Keved, gifted at drawing
becomes Jasnah Kholin's ward in Kharbranth, secretly meaning to steal her Soulcaster to save her family
swaps Jasnah's Soulcaster for a fake, only to find the device dead in her hands
can Soulcast without a fabrial and glimpses the strange symbol-headed Cryptics, hints of her own buried power
Gavilar's widow and Elhokar's mother, a brilliant artifabrian who toys with fabrial science
draws close to Dalinar, helping him test whether his stormvisions are real or a sickness
an old, scorned book of royal philosophy by Nohadon that Dalinar has Navani's scholars read to him
its parable of the Way of Kings shapes Dalinar's resolve to act with honour even when it looks weak
the savage battle-joy Alethi warriors chase in combat; Dalinar grows uneasy as he feels it fade in him
gems set in glass that hold Stormlight, serving as both money and light across Roshar
recharged by being left out in a highstorm, slowly going dun as their light leaks away
Szeth-son-son-Vallano, Truthless of Shinovar, bound to obey whoever holds his Oathstone
wields a stolen Honorblade, Surgebinding through Lashings to walk walls and fly, the Assassin in White
kills on command and hates every death, passed from master to master across the kingdoms
Numuhukumakiaki'aialunamor, a hulking Horneater cook of Bridge Four with a booming laugh
can see spren others cannot, and quietly notices the windspren that follows Kaladin
Dalinar's elder son, a brash, dueling Shardbearer who loves courtship and fine clothes
fiercely loyal to his father even as he fears Dalinar's visions are madness
the slaver who hauls Kaladin across the plains in a cage, ready to abandon his stock to a highstorm
the Blackthorn, Highprince of War and brother to the murdered King Gavilar
during every highstorm he suffers visions of the ancient Desolations and the Knights Radiant
tries to live the ideals of the in-world book The Way of Kings, earning whispers that he has gone soft or mad
the visions command him to unite Alethkar; he wonders whether to trust them or stand down as Highprince
betrayed by Sadeas at the Tower, he survives only because of Kaladin and his bridgemen
a hard, capable bridgeman who becomes one of Kaladin's strongest spearmen and lieutenants

knights-radiant.title: Knights Radiant

points.text: the legendary orders of Surgebinders who once defended humanity, bound to spren and oaths
points.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: they abandoned their oaths and vanished in the Recreance, leaving only their dead Shards behind
Kaladin and Shallan show, unknowing, that the orders are beginning to return

knights-radiant.chapter: Prelude · To the Edge of the World

points.text: Shallan's eldest brother, who vanished from House Davar carrying secret allegiances
tiny spirits that gather around emotions, ideas and forces: rotspren on wounds, painspren, windspren and more
the rarer, intelligent kinds like honorspren and Cryptics can bond a person and grant Radiant powers
a shadowy secret society pursuing hidden ends, tied to Shallan's brother Helaran
the city of bells ruled by Taravangian, home to the Palanaeum, the greatest library in the world
the ten near-independent lords of Alethkar, each running his own war camp and chasing his own glory
their rivalry and refusal to cooperate is exactly what Dalinar's visions command him to end
the Blade of one of the Heralds, which grants Szeth his Surgebinding powers when held
a girl of Hearthstone Kaladin once expected to marry, later promised to Roshone's household instead
Kaladin's gentle younger brother, who could always make him smile with a carved wooden figure
conscripted into the army and killed in his first battle; Kaladin enlisted to protect him and failed
the spiteful citylord sent to Hearthstone, whose feud with Lirin helped drive Kaladin and Tien to the army
a worldly Azish bridgeman, once apprenticed to a Worldsinger, who keeps facts and tallies for Kaladin
Kaladin's father, the surgeon of Hearthstone, who wanted his son to heal rather than kill
clashes with Brightlord Roshone over a hoard of spheres meant for Kaladin's surgeon training
the cliff where broken bridgemen leap to their deaths; Kaladin goes to it but chooses to live instead
the king who united Alethkar, murdered by Szeth on the night a treaty was signed with the Parshendi
dying, he gives Szeth a strange sphere and the words 'You must find the most important words a man can say'
the Alethi oath to punish the Parshendi for Gavilar's assassination, the reason for the long war
the kindly, slow-witted king of Kharbranth who builds hospitals for the poor
secretly commands Szeth through his Oathstone, a far colder schemer than his gentle face suggests
Dalinar's younger son, kept from battle by a blood weakness and a quiet, watchful mind
the magic of binding the fundamental Surges of nature, powered by Stormlight and a spren bond
Lashings let a Surgebinder change the pull of gravity, sticking things together or sending them flying
Elhokar's sister and the king's scholar, a famous heretic who denies the Almighty
researches the Voidbringers and the lost Knights Radiant, certain a Desolation is returning
Soulcasts four would-be muggers to crystal and smoke in a back alley, revealing terrifying power
the proud warrior kingdom Gavilar united, now ruled by Elhokar from the distant capital Kholinar
the radiant energy carried by highstorms and stored in gems, fuel for fabrials and Surgebinding alike
Kaladin can draw it into himself to heal and move impossibly fast, glowing with held light
an ancient sword that can be summoned in ten heartbeats and cuts anything, killing the soul of the living
relics of the lost Knights Radiant, now prized spoils that make their bearers nearly unstoppable
the god of Vorinism, whom Jasnah openly denies as a heretic while Dalinar clings to faith
a marbled-skin people of the Shattered Plains who sing in battle and claimed responsibility for killing Gavilar
they grow forms and bond with their own kind of spren, deeper and stranger than the Alethi suspect
a vast maze of plateaus split by deep chasms, where the Alethi war camps besiege the Parshendi
armies race across on bridges to contest gemhearts in giant chrysalises after highstorms
the lighteyed lord Kaladin once admired and served as a spearman
murders Kaladin's squad and brands him a slave to steal the Shardblade Kaladin won, the wound behind Kaladin's hatred
the small Alethi town where Kaladin grew up as the surgeon's son
an honorspren who attaches herself to Kaladin, at first a flitting windspren he barely notices
grows in mind and memory as Kaladin keeps his oaths, becoming his companion and conscience
can become a weapon for him, the bond that makes him a Knight Radiant reborn
the nervous, one-eyed sergeant over the bridge crews who first torments and then fears Kaladin
a grizzled older bridgeman who becomes Kaladin's first true follower and trains the others in the spear
raised among the Envisagers, he recognises the signs of the Radiants returning in Kaladin
a one-armed Herdazian bridgeman, irrepressibly cheerful and always cracking jokes

relationships.label: abandoned in the Recreance by the
